movies.title: Panic Carefully
movies.notes: 26 February 2027 **+ Shot entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras. Aspect ratio opens up to 1.43.1 entirely at IMAX GT venues and 1.90.1 at IMAX Digital venues. The movie happens to be the first film shot on IMAX digital cameras that opens up to 1.43.1 for the entire runtime.

movies.title: Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse
movies.notes: 18 June 2027 **+ Aspect ratio opens up to 1.43:1 for the entire film. Home media release to retain IMAX aspect ratio on Sony Pictures Core .

movies.title: Avengers: Doomsday
movies.notes: 18 December 2026 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Selected territories only. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film.

movies.title: Avengers: Endgame Encore
movies.notes: 25 September 2026 **+ Originally released in 2019. Re-release featuring "custom introduction, additional new footage, and a special end tag exclusive to Infinity Vision and IMAX releases." Aspect ratio opens up to 1.90:1 for the entire film.

movies.title: Godzilla Minus Zero
movies.notes: 3 November 2026 **+ Filmed partially with IMAX-certified digital cameras. Aspect ratio opens up to 1.43.1 at IMAX GT Laser venues and 1.90.1 at IMAX digital venues.

movies.title: Mercy
movies.notes: 23 January 2026 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 60 minutes. Enhanced framing technique known as "Frame-Break" used to enhance CGI effects, making effects travel "outside" the screen.

movies.title: Mortal Kombat II
movies.notes: 8 May 2026 **+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 45 minutes of the film.

movies.title: Project Hail Mary
movies.notes: 20 March 2026 ***+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.43:1 at IMAX 70mm and IMAX GT Laser venues and 1.85:1 (with Pillarboxing / Windowboxing) at IMAX Digital venues for 75% of the film. An exclusive 5-minute prologue preview of The Odyssey was attached, with the prologue being shown in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio at select IMAX GT Venues (Film Only) as well as a 1.43.1 trailer for Dune: Part Three (Film & Laser). Re-released on April 17 at select IMAX locations. Home-Media retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Resident Evil
movies.notes: 18 September 2026 **+ Filmed partially with IMAX-certified digital cameras. Aspect ratio opens up to 1.90:1.

movies.title: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu
movies.notes: 22 May 2026 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed partially with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.43:1 for 55 minutes of the film at IMAX GT Laser Venues and 1.90:1 for IMAX Digital Venues. The pre-show included a special version of the IMAX Countdown.

movies.title: Street Fighter
movies.notes: 16 October 2026 **+ Filmed partially with IMAX-certified digital cameras. Aspect ratio opens up to 1.90:1.

movies.title: Supergirl
movies.notes: 26 June 2026 **+ Released in 2D in select territories. Released in 3D (conversion) in other territories. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras. Aspect ratio opens up to 1.90:1 for 52 minutes.

movies.title: The Bride!
movies.notes: 6 March 2026 **+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 55 minutes. 15 minutes of the film also opened up to 1.43:1 in IMAX GT Laser venues.

movies.title: The End of Oak Street
movies.notes: 14 August 2026 **+ Filmed partially with IMAX-certified digital cameras. Aspect ratio opens up to 1.90:1.

movies.title: The Odyssey
movies.notes: 17 July 2026 ***+ First feature film to be shot entirely with IMAX 65mm cameras. Presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio on 70mm Film at IMAX GT Venues and Digital at IMAX GT Laser Venues. Presented in 1.90:1 for IMAX Digital Venues. Home media retained the expanded aspect ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Avatar: Fire and Ash
movies.notes: 19 December 2025 **+ In 3D and Variable HFR (48fps) 3D. Presented in a theatrical 1.85:1 aspect ratio across all IMAX Venues (with Pillarboxing / Windowboxing). An exclusive 5-minute prologue preview of The Odyssey was attached, with the prologue being shown in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio at select IMAX GT Venues (Film & Laser). The pre-show included a special version ( Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu ) of the IMAX Countdown.

movies.title: F1
movies.notes: 27 June 2025 **+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film.

movies.title: Final Destination Bloodlines
movies.notes: 16 May 2025 **+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 50 minutes.

movies.pageTitle: How to Train Your Dragon (2025 film)
movies.title: How to Train Your Dragon (2025)
movies.notes: 13 June 2025 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 50 minutes. Home media retained the expanded aspect ratio for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning
movies.notes: 23 May 2025 **+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified Sony CineAlta VENICE cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 45 minutes. Home media retained the expanded aspect ratio for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: One Battle After Another
movies.notes: 26 September 2025 ***+ Presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio on 70mm Film at IMAX GT Venues and Digital at IMAX GT Laser Venues. Presented in 1.90:1 for IMAX Digital Venues. Shot in full-frame VistaVision, it is the first feature-length narrative film presented entirely in 1.43:1 aspect ratio.

movies.title: Sinners
movies.notes: 18 April 2025 ***+ 25 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras. Presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues with 70mm Film & Dual Laser, and 1.90:1 for IMAX Digital and IMAX with Laser. Home media retained the expanded aspect ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Superman
movies.notes: 11 July 2025 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film.

movies.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites
movies.notes: 5 September 2025 **+ Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 10 minutes. Home media retained the expanded aspect ratio in an open matte 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: The Fantastic Four: First Steps
movies.notes: 25 July 2025 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film. 5 minutes of the film also opened up to 1.43:1 in IMAX GT Laser Venues. The pre-show included a special version of the IMAX Countdown. Home media retained the expanded aspect ratio for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ with the 1.43:1 IMAX scenes in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9).

movies.title: The Smashing Machine
movies.notes: 22 September 2025 **+ Limited engagement. Shot with IMAX 65mm cameras. Ending opened up to 1.43.1 in all theaters.

movies.title: Thunderbolts*
movies.notes: 2 May 2025 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opening up to 1.90:1 for 40 minutes. The pre-show included a special version ( The Fantastic Four: First Steps ) of the IMAX Countdown.

movies.title: Tron: Ares
movies.notes: 10 October 2025 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 50% of the film. Home-Media retained the expanded aspect ratio for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+

movies.title: Dune: Part Two
movies.notes: 1 March 2024 ***+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified Arri Alexa 65 and Alexa Mini LF cameras. Expanded aspect ratio of 1.90:1 at IMAX Digital Venues. Select scenes in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio at IMAX GT Venues (Both Laser and 70mm). Released in ten 70mm locations worldwide.

movies.pageTitle: Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire
movies.title: Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire
movies.notes: 29 March 2024 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 45 minutes.

movies.title: Joker: Folie à Deux
movies.notes: 4 October 2024 ***+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified Arri Alexa 65 and Alexa Mini LF cameras. Also released on IMAX 70mm. 55 minutes of footage in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio at IMAX GT Venues (Both Laser and 70mm) and 1.90:1 aspect ratio at IMAX Digital Venues. Home-Media retained the expanded Aspect Ratio for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Megalopolis
movies.notes: 27 September 2024 **+ 5 minutes of expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio scenes at select IMAX GT Laser venues.

movies.title: Venom: The Last Dance
movies.notes: 25 October 2024 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with aspect ratio opening up to 1.90:1 for 40 minutes and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Sony Pictures Core .

movies.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania
movies.notes: 17 February 2023 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified cameras, presented in 1.90:1 for 35 minutes and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom
movies.notes: 22 December 2023 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified cameras with aspect ratio opened up to 1.78:1 for the entire film and preserved on Home-Media.

movies.title: Blue Beetle
movies.notes: 18 August 2023 **+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified cameras with an aspect ratio opening up to 1.90:1 for 70 minutes.

movies.title: Creed III
movies.notes: 3 March 2023 **+ Select-sequences filmed with IMAX-certified Sony CineAlta VENICE cameras, presented in 1.90:1 for approximately 26 minutes.

movies.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
movies.notes: 5 May 2023 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified Red V-Raptor cameras. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D (in Japan) & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: Oppenheimer
movies.notes: 21 July 2023 ***+ 40 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras. The first film to shoot sections in IMAX black-and-white analog photography . Presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio on IMAX 70mm at IMAX GT Venues and Digital at IMAX GT Laser Venues. Presented in 1.90:1 for IMAX Digital Venues. The longest single-platter IMAX film to date, at 180 minutes. Home-Media (excl Streaming & DVD) retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: The Marvels
movies.notes: 10 November 2023 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 30 minutes and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: Avatar: The Way of Water
movies.notes: 16 December 2022 **+ In 3D and Variable HFR (48fps) 3D. Presented in a theatrical 1.85:1 aspect ratio across all IMAX Venues (with Pillarboxing / Windowboxing). Behind the Scenes of Mission: Impossible – Dead Reckoning Part One , Creed III were attached and shown in an expanded aspect ratio of 1.90:1, as well as an exclusive preview for Oppenheimer , which was shown in an expanded aspect ratio of 1.43:1 in select IMAX theaters. Re-released 3 October 2025 in 4K HDR, with select scenes in HFR (48fps).

movies.pageTitle: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
movies.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
movies.notes: 6 May 2022 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras. An exclusive 5-minute preview of Top Gun: Maverick was attached and was shown in the expanded aspect ratio of 1.90:1 and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+.

movies.title: Lightyear
movies.notes: 17 June 2022 **+ Released in 2D in select territories. Released in 3D in other territories. The first American CGI-animated feature film to have its expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio for 25 minutes of the film at IMAX GT Laser Venues and 1.90:1 aspect ratio at IMAX Digital Venues, and preserved on home media in the latter for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+. The first film to have a new IMAX virtual camera in its production.

movies.title: Nope
movies.notes: 22 July 2022 ***+ 50 minutes filmed with IMAX 65mm cameras. Home-Media (excl Streaming) retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
movies.notes: 8 July 2022 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 35 minutes and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+.

movies.title: Top Gun: Maverick
movies.notes: 27 May 2022 **+ Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 60 minutes. Re-released in IMAX on 24–30 June. Re-released again in IMAX on 12 August. Home-Media retained the expanded Aspect Ratio for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Uncharted
movies.notes: 18 February 2022 **+ Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 20 minutes and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Sony Pictures Core .

movies.title: Black Widow
movies.notes: 9 July 2021 **+ Released in 2D in select territories. Released in 3D (conversion) in other territories. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 20 minutes of the film and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D (in Japan) & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ . Released simultaneously in theaters and on Disney+ with Premier Access.

movies.title: Dune
movies.notes: 16 September 2021 ***+ Released in 2D in North America and select territories. Released in 3D (conversion) in other territories. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified Arri Alexa LF and Arri Alexa Mini LF . First American feature film to be presented in IMAX Digital 1.43:1. Expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio at IMAX GT Laser Venues, and 1.90:1 aspect ratio at IMAX Laser and IMAX Digital Venues with 60 minutes of expanded footage. Debuted at the Toronto International Film Festival in IMAX at the Ontario Place Cinesphere on 11 September 2021. Released in the US on 22 October simultaneously in theaters and on HBO Max . Re-released in IMAX on 3 December.

movies.title: Eternals
movies.notes: 5 November 2021 **+ Released in 2D in North America and select territories. Released in 3D (conversion) in other territories. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified Arri Alexa LF and Arri Alexa Mini LF cameras with an aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for approximately 65 minutes. 5 minutes of the film also opened up to 1.43:1 in IMAX GT Laser venues. Home-Media retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in both 1.90:1 and 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes in IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: No Time to Die
movies.notes: 30 September 2021 **+ Released in 2D in North America and select territories. Released in 3D (conversion) in other territories. 40 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras and an additional 10 minutes of scenes filmed on 15-perf 70mm. Released in IMAX Digital formats in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio at IMAX GT Laser Venues and 1.90:1 aspect ratio for IMAX Laser & Digital Venues. Re-released in IMAX on 21 January 2022.

movies.pageTitle: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
movies.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
movies.notes: 3 September 2021 **+ Released in 2D in North America and select territories. Released in 3D (conversion) in other territories. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified Arri Alexa LF and Arri Alexa Mini LF cameras. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
movies.notes: 17 December 2021 **+ Released in 2D in North America and select territories. Released in 3D (conversion) in other territories. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified Arri Alexa LF and Arri Alexa Mini LF cameras. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Sony Pictures Core .

10 January 2020 **+ Limited engagement. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film. The DMR process was not used for the film; instead, a custom transfer was made. Opened in select IMAX theaters in North America on 10 January 2020 before expanding nationwide on 24 January 2020.

movies.title: Tenet
movies.notes: 26 August 2020 ***+ 75 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras. Presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT venues with 70mm Film & Dual Laser, and 1.90:1 for IMAX Digital and IMAX Laser. Home-Media (excl Streaming) retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes. The IMAX 70mm version debuted in ten theaters globally. The pre-show included a special version of the IMAX countdown.

movies.title: Wonder Woman 1984
movies.notes: 16 December 2020 **+ Released in 2D in North America. Released in 3D (conversion) in selected territories. 15 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras in select scenes opened up to a 1.90:1 aspect ratio. Released in the US on 25 December simultaneously in theaters and on HBO Max .

movies.title: Alita: Battle Angel
movies.notes: 14 February 2019 **+ In 3D and 2D. Filmed with the Fusion Camera System . Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 40 minutes of the film. Re-released in select North American IMAX theaters on 28 October 2020.

movies.title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
movies.notes: 2 July 2019 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 45 minutes of the film and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Sony Pictures Core . Re-released as an extended cut on 30 August with aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 50 minutes of the film.

movies.pageTitle: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
movies.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
movies.notes: 20 December 2019 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. An exclusive 5-minute prologue preview of Tenet was attached at select IMAX theaters, with the prologue being shown in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio at select IMAX GT Venues (Film & Laser) including 5 in the US and one in the UK.

movies.title: The Lion King (2019)
movies.notes: 19 July 2019 **+ In 3D and 2D. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.43:1 for 25 minutes of musical sequences on select IMAX with Laser GT Venues.

movies.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp
movies.notes: 6 July 2018 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 30 minutes of the film and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D (in the UK) & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ . Exclusive preview of Mission: Impossible – Fallout attached.

movies.title: Avengers: Infinity War
movies.notes: 27 April 2018 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. First American feature film to be entirely filmed with IMAX Arri Alexa cameras equipped with Panavision Sphero 65 and Ultra Panavision 70 lenses in 3D rigs. Expanded 1.90:1 aspect ratio for entire runtime and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: First Man
movies.notes: 12 October 2018 ***+ 10-minute Lunar Sequence shot with IMAX 65mm cameras and presented in a 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues with Laser and 1.90:1 aspect ratio for IMAX Digital. Debuted at the Toronto International Film Festival in IMAX at the Ontario Place Cinesphere on 9 September 2018. Home-Media retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for the Lunar Sequence.

movies.title: Iron Man
movies.notes: 30 August 2018 **+ Originally released in 2008. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for select sequences and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ . A part of the Marvel Studios 10th Anniversary IMAX Film Festival. Limited engagement.

movies.title: Baahubali 2: The Conclusion
movies.notes: 28 April 2017 **+ First IMAX DMR Telugu-language release. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film. India and selected territories only. Limited engagement.

movies.title: Blade Runner 2049
movies.notes: 6 October 2017 **+ Released in 2D in North America and South Korea. Released in 3D (conversion) in selected territories. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film. The pre-show included a special version of the IMAX Countdown. The DMR process was not used for the film, instead a custom transfer was made.

movies.title: Dunkirk
movies.notes: 21 July 2017 ***+ 80 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras. IMAX 70mm version shown in 35 theaters globally. Presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues (70mm & Laser) and 1.90:1 aspect ratio for IMAX Digital. Home-Media (excl Streaming) retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
movies.notes: 5 May 2017 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 40 minutes of the film and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D (for the Best Buy Exclusive 4K Steelbook) & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ . Enhanced framing technique known as "Frame-Break" used to enhance 3D effects, making effects travel "outside" the screen.

movies.title: Thor: Ragnarok
movies.notes: 3 November 2017 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 40 minutes of the film and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D (in the UK) & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: Transformers: The Last Knight
movies.notes: 21 June 2017 **+ In 3D. Filmed entirely with IMAX Arri Alexa cameras in 3D rigs for 100% of IMAX 3D footage. Aspect ratio alternates between 1.90:1, 2.00:1, and 2.39:1 throughout the entire film.

movies.pageTitle: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
movies.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
movies.notes: 25 March 2016 ***+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. 25 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras. Presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues and 1.90:1 aspect ratio for IMAX Digital. 2D 70mm version released on select screens.

movies.title: Captain America: Civil War
movies.notes: 6 May 2016 ***+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. First American feature to be partially filmed with IMAX Arri Alexa cameras with 15 minutes of IMAX footage, presented in a 1.90:1 aspect ratio and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: Doctor Strange
movies.notes: 4 November 2016 **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 60 minutes of the film and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: Sully
movies.notes: 9 September 2016 ***+ Filmed entirely with IMAX Arri Alexa cameras. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for the entire film. The pre-show included a special version of the IMAX Countdown.

movies.title: Star Wars: The Force Awakens
movies.notes: 18 December 2015 ***+ In 3D (conversion). 5-minute Millennium Falcon pursuit sequence shot with IMAX 65mm cameras with 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues and 1.90:1 aspect ratio for IMAX Digital.

movies.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
movies.notes: 1 August 2014 **+ In 3D (conversion). Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 35 minutes of the film and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ . Enhanced framing technique known as "Frame-Break" used to enhance CGI effects, making effects travel "outside" the screen.

movies.title: Interstellar
movies.notes: 7 November 2014 ***+ 65 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras. Presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues and 1.90:1 for IMAX Digital. Home-Media (excl Streaming & DVD) retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes. The final worldwide IMAX 70mm DMR release for three years. Closing credits changed to slide format, and one closing credits song was omitted to reduce running time for IMAX 70mm prints. Re-released on 6 December 2024 for its 10th anniversary.

movies.title: Transformers: Age of Extinction
movies.notes: 27 June 2014 **+ In 3D. Filmed with Phantom 65 IMAX 3D Digital cameras. Select scenes filmed in 35mm and converted to 3D. First American feature to be partially filmed with IMAX 3D digital cameras with 95 minutes of IMAX 3D footage. Aspect ratio alternates between 2.39:1, 2.00:1, and 1.90:1 and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D. The longest IMAX 3D movie ever filmed.

movies.title: Star Trek Into Darkness
movies.notes: 17 May 2013 ***+ In 3D (conversion). 30 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras, in expanded 1.66:1 and 1.90:1 aspect ratios for IMAX GT and IMAX Digital Venues.

movies.title: James Cameron's Titanic
movies.notes: 4 April 2012 *** In 3D (conversion). Originally released in 1997 with a 2.39:1 aspect ratio, opened up to 1.78:1 for this presentation and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D . First IMAX DMR film to surpass both 180 and 190 minutes. For IMAX 70mm prints, the film was split into 2 parts due to platter size limits, with the first part running approximately 108 minutes and the second part running approximately 87 minutes with a 15-minute intermission in between. The longest IMAX 3D DMR film to date. Re-released in HFR (48fps) 3D on 10 February 2023, for its 25th anniversary. At 194 minutes, Titanic is the longest IMAX 3D release of all time as of October 2023.

movies.title: The Dark Knight Rises
movies.notes: 20 July 2012 ***+ 70 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras, in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues. Home-Media (excl Streaming & DVD) retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes. The pre-show included a special version of the IMAX Countdown.

movies.title: Mission: Impossible – Ghost Protocol
movies.notes: 16 December 2011 ***+ 30 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras, presented in a 1.43:1 aspect ratio at IMAX GT Venues. Six-minute preview of The Dark Knight Rises attached to IMAX 70mm prints.

movies.title: Tron: Legacy
movies.notes: 17 December 2010 ***+ In 3D. Filmed with the Fusion Camera System . 45 minutes printed with a 1.78:1 aspect ratio for IMAX 70mm prints and 1.90:1 for IMAX Digital. Preserved on home media in 1.78:1 (16:9) aspect ratio.

movies.title: James Cameron's Avatar
movies.notes: 18 December 2009 ***+ In 3D (conversion). Exclusive 1.78:1 aspect ratio. Filmed with the Fusion Camera System . Screened with no end credits in some theaters with older IMAX equipment which had platter size limits. Re-released 23 September 2022 in 4K HDR, with select scenes in HFR (48fps).

movies.title: Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen
movies.notes: 24 June 2009 ***+ 10 minutes shot with IMAX 65mm cameras, presented in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio. Preserved on home media in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) aspect ratio for the Walmart "Big Screen Edition". About half-a-minute of footage exclusive to the IMAX version.

movies.title: Kung Fu Panda
movies.notes: 6 June 2008 *** Aspect ratio cropped to 1.43:1 for IMAX 70mm prints.

movies.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
movies.notes: 7 November 2008 *** Aspect ratio cropped to 1.43:1 for IMAX 70mm prints.

movies.title: The Dark Knight
movies.notes: 18 July 2008 ***+ First American feature to be partially shot with IMAX 65mm cameras, with 30 minutes of footage in an expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues. First DMR film to surpass 150 minutes. Home-Media (excl Streaming & DVD) retained the expanded Aspect Ratio in a cropped 1.78:1 (16:9) for IMAX scenes.

movies.title: Happy Feet
movies.notes: 17 November 2006 * Aspect ratio cropped to 1.43:1 for IMAX 70mm prints.

movies.title: The Ant Bully
movies.notes: 28 July 2006 * In 3D. Aspect ratio cropped to 1.43:1 for IMAX 70mm prints.

movies.title: Apollo 13
movies.notes: 20 September 2002 * The first IMAX DMR film. Originally released in 1995. The 2002 re-release is 25 minutes shorter than the 35mm version due to older IMAX equipment having platter size limits, with these scenes remastered for the 2025 re-release for its 30th anniversary. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.66:1 for the 2002 re-release.

movies.pageTitle: Star Wars: Episode II – Attack of the Clones
movies.title: Star Wars: Episode II – Attack of the Clones
movies.notes: 1 November 2002 * Originally released in May 2002. 20 minutes shorter than the 35mm version due to older IMAX equipment having platter size limits. Aspect ratio cropped to 1.85:1. Closing credits changed to slide format due to platter size limits.

movies.title: Black Panther
movies.notes: **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Aspect ratio opened up to 1.90:1 for 50 minutes of the film and preserved on home media for Blu-ray 3D (in the UK) & IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ .

movies.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
movies.notes: **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opening up to 1.90:1 for 20 minutes and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+.

movies.title: Captain America: Brave New World
movies.notes: **+ In 3D (conversion) and 2D. Filmed entirely with IMAX-certified digital cameras with an aspect ratio opening up to 1.90:1 for 30 minutes and preserved on home media for IMAX Enhanced on Disney+ . The pre-show included a special version ( The Fantastic Four: First Steps ) of the IMAX Countdown.

movies.title: Dune: Part Three
movies.notes: ***+ Shot partially with IMAX 65mm cameras with some scenes expanded to 1.43:1 at IMAX 70mm GT venues, IMAX Laser GT venues, and 1.90:1 at IMAX Digital Venues.

movies.title: Ghostbusters
movies.notes: **+ In 3D (conversion). Enhanced framing technique known as "Frame-Break" used to enhance CGI effects, making effects travel "outside" the screen. 1 minute of emotional "Portal" scene opened up to 1.43:1 aspect ratio for IMAX GT Venues and 1.90:1 aspect ratio for IMAX Digital.
